channel.title: Visual-First on The Independent PA
channel.description: Recent content in Visual-First on The Independent PA

item.title: The $600k Exit: A Paly Student Guide
item.author: newsroom@independentpa.local (The Independent PA Newsroom)
item.description:
What happened: In March 2026, PAUSD approved a separation package totaling $596,802 tied to Superintendent Don Austin’s resignation.

Visual-First Carousel Script

Slide 1 — The $596,802 Exit

  • Headline: THE $596,802 EXIT
  • Subhead: March 2026: PAUSD approved a separation package totaling $596,802.
  • Paly Angle: Big district financial decisions set the context for student-facing priorities this semester.

Slide 2 — Follow the Money

  • Headline: FOLLOW THE MONEY
  • Subhead: Breakdown: approximately 17 months of salary continuation + health benefits.
  • Paly Angle: Breaking down the total keeps campus conversation factual, not rumor-driven.

Slide 3 — Opportunity Cost

  • Headline: WHAT ELSE COULD THIS FUND?
  • Subhead: Opportunity-cost modeling translates $596,802 into student-facing equivalents.
  • Paly Angle: Students can compare abstract district spending to concrete supports they recognize on campus.

Slide 4 — Transition

  • Headline: WHO LEADS NOW?
  • Subhead: District moved to acting-superintendent leadership while permanent decisions continue.
  • Paly Angle: Leadership transitions can shift the timeline on decisions students track.

Slide 5 — What’s Next

  • Headline: WHAT STUDENTS SHOULD WATCH NEXT
  • Subhead: Watch board dates, budget checkpoints, and student-service decisions.
  • Paly Angle: The real value is tracking what happens after the headline.

3-Slide Quick Read

1) What happened

PAUSD approved a separation payout totaling $596,802 in March 2026.
